key_insight: Raising the barrel filling ratio from 43% to 53% produced a denser Cr3C2–NiCr coating and improved wear resistance, even though friction increased.
engineering_relevance: Optimizing detonation-spraying fill ratio is a practical lever for improving the durability of carbide-based protective coatings in severe wear applications.
method: Detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coatings were deposited at two barrel filling ratios: 43% and 53%.
Microstructure and phases were analyzed by microscopy and X-ray diffraction.
Dry ball-on-disc tribological tests were run at 10 and 15 N with 5 and 10 cm/s sliding speeds.
evidence_signal: The 53% fill coating showed the lowest reported wear rate, 1.23 × 10^-4 mm3/(m·N), alongside reduced porosity and better structural integrity.
watch_item: The improved wear resistance came with a higher coefficient of friction, so system-level performance tradeoffs should be checked before implementation.
paper_title: Effect of Barrel Filling Ratio on the Microstructure, Phase Composition and Tribological Performance of Detonation-Sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr Coatings
